A.name: Path A: First Playable Game
A.audience: Absolute beginners and learners returning to Roblox development.
A.outcome: A small, publicly playable game loop.

milestones.title: Choose a simple game loop and player fantasy
milestones.why: Every good game starts with a clear hook and loop before code is written.

milestones.title: Set up the Studio project and spawn a visible interactive object
milestones.why: You need a physical anchor in the world for players to interact with.

milestones.title: Track collected items in local arrays/dictionaries
milestones.why: Before sending anything to the server, understand how to model collections in memory.

milestones.title: Capture an interaction and validate the reward on the server
milestones.why: Server authority prevents players from granting themselves infinite currency.

milestones.title: Show progress in a simple UI
milestones.why: Players need immediate feedback when their state changes.

milestones.title: Make state survive rejoin via safe persistence
milestones.why: Without saving, players lose all progress when they leave.

milestones.title: Clamp multiplier rewards and coin limits
milestones.why: Clamping values prevents integers from overflowing or players getting infinite coins from bugs.

milestones.title: Test first-session flow on desktop and mobile
milestones.why: Mobile makes up over half of Roblox players; layout must fit all screens.

rounds.name: Round-based loop
rounds.description: lobby, server-owned round state, win condition, reset, simple reward.

milestones.title: Choose a round structure and player goal
milestones.why: Every round-based game needs a lobby waiting area, a game state, and clear victory rules.

milestones.title: Set up the Studio project and lobby spawn
milestones.why: Players need a safe area to wait while a round is not in progress.

milestones.title: Define round state using tables
milestones.why: Keep track of players alive, time left, and game status (Lobby, Active, Intermission).

milestones.title: Transition rounds on the server using RemoteEvents
milestones.why: The server must control when rounds start and end to prevent client hacks.

milestones.title: Show round status and timer in UI
milestones.why: Players must see how much time is left in a round.

milestones.title: Save wins or round rewards securely
milestones.why: Wins must be saved so players can buy lobby cosmetic gear or titles.

milestones.title: Clamp round timer and speed settings
milestones.why: Clamping values prevents time from running negative or players moving impossibly fast.

milestones.title: Test multi-player rounds and publish
milestones.why: Round transition bugs (like double rounds or missing resets) only show when multiple players join.

quest.name: Exploration / quest loop
quest.description: interaction, state flag, UI feedback, persistence, return objective.

milestones.title: Choose a quest storyline and player objectives
milestones.why: A quest must have a clear hook and conditions for success.

milestones.title: Set up Studio project and quest NPC dialog
milestones.why: Players need to talk to NPCs to start the quest.

milestones.title: Track quest states in a local dictionary
milestones.why: Model active, complete, and failed states for multiple quest goals.

milestones.title: Trigger quest completions via server validation
milestones.why: Server validation prevents players from instantly triggering the end of a quest.

milestones.title: Display active objectives on a HUD
milestones.why: Players need to know what they are supposed to do next.

milestones.title: Save quest completion flags securely
milestones.why: Save quest states so players don't have to repeat quests.

milestones.title: Limit quest rewards and cooldown timer
milestones.why: Cooldowns prevent players from spamming quests and flooding the economy.

milestones.title: Test quest flow and publish
milestones.why: Quest state transitions (NPC talk, gather items, return) must work end-to-end.

B.name: Path B: Game Systems Builder
B.audience: Learners who have shipped or completed a first vertical slice.

milestones.title: Client/server ownership and RemoteEvent validation
milestones.why: Securing network communications is key to multiplayer design.

milestones.title: Authoritative currencies, inventory, and progression
milestones.why: Keep progression secure from client manipulations.

milestones.title: UI state, input, and feedback
milestones.why: Decouple client render state from server logic.

milestones.title: Modular architecture and service boundaries
milestones.why: Keeps your codebase clean as game size scales.

milestones.title: Persistence schemas, failure paths, and safe changes
milestones.why: Prevent data loss when updating player schema versions.

milestones.title: Rounds, matchmaking, tools, or genre modules
milestones.why: Structure round management separately from player session state.

milestones.title: Profiling, debugging, and release checks
milestones.why: Verify performance targets before opening to the public.

C.name: Path C: Creator Operator
C.audience: Builders with a functioning small game.
C.outcome: A creator who can run thoughtful iterations rather than blindly add features.

milestones.title: First-session clarity and time-to-fun
milestones.why: Hook players within the first minute or they will bounce.

milestones.title: Retention and funnel interpretation
milestones.why: Understand where players get stuck using structured events.

milestones.title: Ethical economy and monetization trade-offs
milestones.why: Build purchases based on real value, not coercive design.

milestones.title: Performance and acquisition quality
milestones.why: A lagging game ruins player retention immediately.

milestones.title: Experiments with one changed variable
milestones.why: Establish baseline hypotheses before coding new features.

milestones.title: Release notes, rollback thinking, and project narrative
milestones.why: Mitigate live risks by planning rollback gates in advance.
